description: Identifies SQS cost inefficiencies from short polling, excessive empty receives, missing batching, and retry/visibility behavior while preserving latency and consumer safety.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: SQS charges per API request. Empty ReceiveMessage responses are billable. Long polling can reduce empty responses, but does not guarantee zero empty receives.

severity_rules.name: short_polling_empty_receives
severity_rules.condition: receive_wait_time_seconds == 0 AND (empty_receives_avg_per_hour >= empty_receives_per_hour_min OR empty_receives_per_day >= empty_receives_per_day_min OR empty_receive_ratio_pct >= empty_receive_ratio_pct_min)
severity_rules.description: Queue uses short polling and has high empty receives. Enable long polling after validating client read timeout, latency requirements, Lambda/SDK behavior, and backlog metrics.

severity_rules.name: long_polling_still_empty
severity_rules.description: Long polling is enabled but empty receives remain high. Review consumer concurrency, polling loop behavior, queue idleness, and event source configuration.

severity_rules.name: batching_opportunity
severity_rules.description: High request volume may benefit from SendMessageBatch, DeleteMessageBatch, or ReceiveMessage MaxNumberOfMessages tuning.

severity_rules.name: retry_visibility_cost_driver
severity_rules.condition: message_age_high == true OR receive_count_high == true OR dlq_messages_high == true OR visibility_timeout_too_low == true
severity_rules.description: Retries, DLQ movement, or visibility timeout issues may be driving repeated receives and request cost.

severity_rules.name: client_timeout_validation_required
severity_rules.description: Client read timeout must exceed long polling wait time to avoid connection errors and ineffective long polling.

savings_calculation.note: Use observed empty receive reduction when available. Otherwise report potential savings with an explicit reduction assumption.

validation_before_change: Check client HTTP/read timeout is greater than receive_wait_time_seconds.
Check latency requirements and consumer behavior.
Check ApproximateAgeOfOldestMessage, visible/not visible messages, DLQ movement, and retries.
Check FIFO message group and throughput behavior where applicable.
reporting: Separate long polling, batching, retry/visibility, and cleanup savings.
Report confidence based on metrics and consumer configuration evidence.
